Home/International Removals/Spain
The Move

Spain is our busiest European route, served by road all year round.

Thousands of British movers head for Spain every year, and we look after a good number of them. Dedicated vehicles leave regularly for the major cities, the Costas and the islands, so your belongings travel with crews who make the journey every week.

Road freight keeps a Spanish move quick and flexible. A dedicated load gives you the fastest door to door service, while a part load shares vehicle space and splits the cost.

Popular destinations we deliver to
MadridBarcelonaValencia MálagaAlicanteMarbella Balearic IslandsCanary Islands
Typical Transit Times
Dedicated road service3–5 days
Part-load (groupage)1–2 weeks
Balearics & Canaries2–3 weeks
Times shown are door to door. Please allow a little extra for customs formalities. We recommend booking 6 to 8 weeks ahead of your move date.
Your options

Choose the right way to move

Every Spanish move is different, so we shape the service around yours.

Road · Dedicated

A vehicle reserved for your move

One crew loads at your door in the UK and the same crew delivers in Spain a few days later. Nothing is transhipped and nothing waits in a depot.

Best for: full households, fixed dates
Road · Part-load

Share the vehicle, share the cost

Your goods travel alongside other Spanish moves on a scheduled departure. A little more patience, a noticeably smaller bill.

Best for: smaller moves, flexible budgets
Islands

Balearics and Canaries covered

We ship regularly to Mallorca, Menorca, Ibiza and the Canary Islands, combining the road and sea legs into one managed journey.

Best for: island relocations
Excess Baggage

A few boxes or suitcases

Not moving a whole household? Our excess baggage service is a simple, affordable way to send personal items and smaller consignments overseas.

Best for: light movers & students
Good to know

Moving to Spain now takes paperwork, and we handle it

UK households entering Spain pass through customs, and the right documents make all the difference. We prepare everything with you well before moving day, so your belongings keep moving.

Customs declarations

Your shipment travels with a full inventory and the declarations Spanish customs expect.

Your NIE number

Most imports need your NIE, the Spanish identification number for foreigners. We will guide you on timing.

Duty relief on household goods

Used personal effects usually enter free of duty when you transfer your main residence to Spain.

Residency evidence

Proof of your move, such as a visa or padrón registration, supports the relief claim.
